prompt:
Fix a port conflict between Caddy and Tailscale Funnel on classic-laddie.

## Problem

Caddy's reverse proxy for media services (Jellyfin, Overseerr) binds to *:443, which
blocks tailscaled from binding its Funnel listener on 100.111.60.17:443. This causes
GitHub webhooks routed through Tailscale Funnel to fail with TLS errors.

The root cause is that Caddy's virtualHosts use bare hostnames with `tls internal`,
which defaults to listening on all interfaces including the Tailscale IP.

## Fix

In `modules/media-server/default.nix`, add `default_bind 192.168.1.28` to the Caddy
globalConfig so Caddy only listens on the LAN interface. This leaves the Tailscale
interface (100.111.60.17) free for tailscaled's Funnel.

The change is at line 173 in the globalConfig:

```nix
globalConfig = ''
  auto_https off
  default_bind 192.168.1.28
'';
```

## Documentation

Add a comment block above the Caddy config section (around line 169) explaining:
- Why default_bind is set to the LAN IP
- That this is required to avoid conflicting with Tailscale Funnel on :443
- That the LAN IP corresponds to the enp4s0 interface on classic-laddie

Also update `docs/media-server-configuration.md` if it exists — add a note about the
Caddy/Tailscale port 443 constraint.

## Acceptance criteria
- Caddy globalConfig includes `default_bind 192.168.1.28`
- Comment explains the why (Tailscale Funnel conflict)
- docs/media-server-configuration.md updated if present
- No other changes to Caddy behavior (same virtualHosts, same tls internal)
